Kraken API Release Notes
========================

v2.4.0 (2026-04-10)
-------------------
- Added support for the X-Kraken-Trace-Id header on every response, carrying
  a unique request identifier suitable for correlation with server logs.
  Operators reported this as the single most-requested observability
  feature in the 2026 Q1 survey.
- The /v2/oauth/token endpoint now accepts a `scope` parameter for
  fine-grained capability requests. Scope values map onto the existing
  permission grid.
- Rate limit headers now include X-Rate-Limit-Bucket-Reset alongside the
  existing X-Rate-Limit-Retry-After. The reset value is a Unix timestamp
  for the bucket's next refill.

v2.3.2 (2026-03-22)
-------------------
- Fixed a long-standing bug where compound queries against /v2/search
  silently dropped a clause when more than five filters were combined.
- API keys created in the dashboard now carry an explicit creation
  timestamp surfaced via /v2/keys/me.

v2.3.0 (2026-02-14)
-------------------
- Tier 3 customers now have access to the streaming endpoint at
  /v2/stream. Connections are upgraded to WebSocket via the standard
  HTTP/1.1 Upgrade dance.

UNIQUE_RELEASE_NOTES_MARKER_TOKEN: this string only appears in the
plain-text release notes; tests use it to verify the text-indexing path
surfaces this document as a retrieval source.
